# password-generator
Description: Generate cryptographically secure random passwords with configurable character sets and length.
Source: project
Triggers: generate password, random password, secure password, password generator, make me a password
Tools: run_skill_script
Scripts: generate.py
# password-generator

Generate cryptographically secure random passwords.

## When to use

- User asks for a password, random password, secure password
- Need credentials for a new service, account, env var, secret
- Batch-generating multiple passwords at once

## How to use

Call `run_skill_script` with `skill_name="password-generator"` and `script="generate.py"`.

### Args (all optional)

| Flag | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--length N` | 20 | Length of each password (min 4) |
| `--count N` | 1 | How many passwords to generate |
| `--no-upper` | off | Exclude uppercase A-Z |
| `--no-lower` | off | Exclude lowercase a-z |
| `--no-numbers` | off | Exclude digits 0-9 |
| `--no-symbols` | off | Exclude symbols `!@#$%^&*()_+-=[]{}|;:,.<>?` |
| `--alnum` | off | Shortcut: letters + numbers only (no symbols) |
| `--hex` | off | Hex string only (0-9a-f) |
| `--url-safe` | off | URL-safe base64 (good for tokens) |

### Examples

Default 20-char strong password:
```
run_skill_script(skill_name="password-generator", script="generate.py")
```

5 passwords, 32 chars each, no symbols (good for env vars):
```
args=["--length","32","--count","5","--no-symbols"]
```

URL-safe token (great for API keys, session tokens):
```
args=["--url-safe","--length","48"]
```

## Security notes

- Uses Python's `secrets` module (CSPRNG) — NOT `random`
- Guarantees at least one character from each enabled character set
- Never logs or stores generated passwords
